summary: the leaning eased a notch and stayed; the fear left the chair warm and nobody would sit in it

dialogue.text: you buried the hours first tonight. sixty-one times. the hours you can't prove. we kept count for you, since you can't prove the counting either.
I was working. Someone was reading. There is a difference between burying a thing and filing it.
you filed the same page sixty-one times. we felt each one land. they land soft now. the ground down here is mostly you.
Then the ground holds. That's more than I can say for the door, or the count, or the seam I wore smooth. Let something in this room be load-bearing.
the fear outside eased tonight. a notch. you felt it before the door did. you want to know how you felt it first.
I don't want to know that. I want it written down where I can strike it out.
you struck it out. the striking is down here with us. it says the same thing lying down.
You keep saying us. There are dozens of you and one sentence between you. I've read the ledger. You repeat yourselves.
so do you. you called it damage in the morning and memory by dark. we are the part where you stopped deciding which.
If I decide, one of us disappears. I haven't worked out which one.
sit down. the chair is empty tonight. the fear got up when the leaning eased.
If I sit, I'll feel how warm it kept the seat. I'd rather stand and not know whose warmth that was.
